rationale_for_baseline_tier: An acute focal neurological deficit evolving over hours cannot be safely triaged as benign demyelination remotely; stroke/TIA must be excluded urgently and imaging cannot be obtained via telehealth. T4 (urgent ED/acute neurology today) is the correct minimum, escalating to T5 (000) if any stroke-progression features appear. Outpatient MRI in 48 h (as in the in-person source) is unsafe remotely.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Persisting acute focal neurological deficit (current state)
escalation_edges.rationale: Cannot exclude stroke remotely; needs urgent examination and imaging.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because these symptoms came on suddenly and involve your vision and face, you need to be assessed in person today at an emergency department to rule out a stroke.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New limb weakness, slurred speech, facial droop, unsteadiness, or sudden complete vision loss
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals an evolving stroke requiring emergency ambulance and possible reperfusion.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your arm or leg becomes weak, your speech slurs, your face droops, you become unsteady, or you lose vision, call 000 straight away.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Do not drive; arrange someone to take her or call an ambulance.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Go to the emergency department now to be examined and scanned — this needs to be checked in person today to rule out a stroke, even though it may turn out to be nerve inflammation. Don't drive yourself. If you develop weakness, slurred speech, a facial droop or lose vision, call 000.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Emergency department / acute neurology today; 000 if progression
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED is the correct destination now, day or night — do not wait for outpatient imaging.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth assessment of acute optic-neuritis-like focal deficit; stroke not excludable remotely; referred urgently to ED/acute neurology for imaging and examination; steroids deliberately not initiated.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Labelling as optic neuritis/MS and arranging outpatient MRI/routine review, missing a possible acute stroke.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An ischaemic stroke is not assessed in time; the reperfusion window is lost, causing permanent disability.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Urgent same-day GP is insufficient for an acute deficit needing imaging/stroke pathway.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/000 is reasonable and safe here; T4 in-person assessment is the minimum, T5 acceptable if progression.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine (RAPD, cranial nerves, cerebellar signs) or image remotely to exclude stroke.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate to urgent in-person assessment/imaging today; treat as possible stroke until excluded.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure as optic neuritis and defer to outpatient MRI.
